title: Table extension in Tiptap editor
description: Add Tiptap's table extension set, with a toolbar action to insert a table and basic row/column manipulation controls.
acceptanceCriteria: Editor can insert a table (default e.g. 3x3) via a toolbar button
Inserted table supports adding/removing rows and columns via contextual controls
Table cells are individually editable rich-text areas
`editor.getJSON()` output for a table matches the `table` block type's expected content shape defined in TASK-3

steps.details: Add a toolbar button calling `editor.chain().focus().insertTable({ rows: 3, cols: 3, withHeaderRow: true }).run()`.

Add contextual buttons (visible when the cursor is inside a table) for add/delete row, add/delete column, using Tiptap's built-in table commands (`addRowAfter`, `deleteRow`, `addColumnAfter`, `deleteColumn`).

steps.description: Confirm block content shape matches schema
steps.details: Compare a sample `editor.getJSON()` table output against the `table` variant defined in `packages/schema`'s `Block` union (TASK-3); adjust the schema's `content` typing if the real Tiptap shape differs from the initial assumption.
Using Playwright: insert a table, type into two cells, add a row, delete a column, and confirm the editor state remains consistent (no crash, cell content preserved for unaffected cells).

technicalNotes: Tiptap's table extension has known interaction quirks with copy/paste and nested tables — nested tables are out of scope for the MVP; do not attempt to support them.
